query.text: What programming language was Unix rewritten in during 1973?

document.content: Python was created by Guido van Rossum and first released in 1991. It emphasizes code readability and uses indentation to define code structure instead of braces.
Java was designed by James Gosling at Sun Microsystems and released in 1995. Its write-once-run-anywhere design enabled cross-platform application development.
FORTRAN, developed by IBM in the 1950s, was the first high-level programming language and was extensively used for scientific and engineering computations.
The C programming language was developed by Dennis Ritchie at Bell Labs. Unix was rewritten in C in 1973, making it the first operating system implemented in a high-level language.

query.text: Who invented the World Wide Web?

document.content: ARPANET, developed in the 1960s by the US Defense Department, was the precursor to the internet and was designed to maintain communications even after a nuclear attack.
Ray Tomlinson is credited with inventing email in 1971, choosing the at-sign to separate usernames from host names in the first networked messaging system.
HTTP, the Hypertext Transfer Protocol, defines how browsers and web servers communicate. HTTP/2 and HTTP/3 deliver major performance improvements over the original specification.
Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web in 1989 while working at CERN, proposing a system of hyperlinked documents to help physicists share information across different computers.

query.text: What element has the chemical symbol Fe on the periodic table?

document.content: Gold, with the chemical symbol Au derived from the Latin word aurum, is a dense yellow metal prized for its rarity, malleability, and resistance to corrosion.
Silver, chemical symbol Ag from the Latin argentum, is a lustrous transition metal used in jewelry, electrical contacts, and as an antimicrobial coating in medical devices.
The periodic table was organized by Dmitri Mendeleev in 1869, arranging elements by atomic weight and revealing repeating patterns in their chemical properties.
Iron, with the chemical symbol Fe from the Latin ferrum, is the most common element on Earth by mass and the primary component of steel alloys widely used in construction.
